content:
- Continued bug task `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-test-bdd-full-suite-hangs.md` from `TO BE VERIFIED`, ran a deep skeptical verification pass across many parallel tracks (code, Makefile, logs, historical evidence, ignored-test inventory), and updated the plan to Draft 2 with a mandatory change: added an overall scenario timeout in addition to per-operation timeouts.
- Switched task marker from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`, then implemented plan in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`:
  - added command timeout constants and helper `wait_for_child_exit_with_timeout` that kills timed-out children,
  - refactored `initialize_pgdata` and `pg_ctl_stop_immediate` to use bounded subprocess waits,
  - wrapped HTTP connect/write/read and worker `step_once` calls with explicit timeout handling,
  - added request-level timeline markers in `send_node_request`,
  - wrapped the full e2e scenario body with a hard timeout and timeline record on timeout.
- Ran all required gates successfully (in required order):
  - `make check` passed.
  - `make test` passed; `ha::e2e_multi_node::e2e_multi_node_real_ha_scenario_matrix` reported the 60s heartbeat and then passed in ~100s.
  - `make test-bdd` passed with same heartbeat behavior then pass.
  - `make lint` passed across all strict clippy passes.
- Updated task file metadata/checklist to done/passing true (`<status>done</status> <passes>true</passes> <passing>true</passing>`), updated AGENTS learnings about heartbeat-vs-hang + explicit timeout guidance, then ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- Next immediate step: stage all modified files (including `.ralph` updates) and commit with required message format; then quit.

- Finalized bug task completion: staged all changes (including `.ralph` artifacts), committed with required message format at commit `3158744`, and verified git working tree is clean.
- Commit includes: task metadata updates, timeout hardening in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`, AGENTS learning update, task switch artifacts, evidence timelines, and progress logs.
- Required gates had already passed before commit: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint`.
- Next step after this handoff: quit this turn immediately unless user requests follow-up changes.

- Ran progress_read (no visible prior output) and read .ralph/current_tasks.md fully.
- Applied task-selection rules: no bug tasks with <passes>false</passes>/<passing>false</passing>; several non-bug tasks are <passes>false</passes>, so they take precedence over the recurring meta-task this cycle.
- Chose .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/26-task-e2e-unassisted-failover-sql-consistency.md as next logical unblocked foundation in remaining e2e chain and wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next agent should execute task 26, then continue through remaining e2e hardening tasks unless a new failing bug appears.

- Read startup memory via `.ralph/progress_read.sh`, loaded `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and opened task `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/26-task-e2e-unassisted-failover-sql-consistency.md`.
- Per workflow gate, performed planning-only pass (no execution): parallel-scanned `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`, API response contracts, and harness SQL capability gaps.
- Confirmed current matrix scenario still performs post-failure control actions (`DELETE /ha/leader`, `POST /ha/leader`) and lacks before/after SQL continuity proof.
- Wrote a full Draft 1 execution plan into task file and appended `TO BE VERIFIED` exactly as requested.
- Stopping immediately now to honor task protocol; next turn should do deep skeptical verification, alter at least one plan item, then switch marker to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Received PO email request to investigate DCS-driving behavior in e2e/integration/full-system-style tests and explicitly avoid running tests.
- Followed mail workflow: sent immediate ack, sent plan email, sent start-subtask email, sent multiple per-finding code emails, sent final narrative report email, and sent final completion email.
- Performed read-only investigation across repo with rg/sed/nl. Scoped tests with e2e/integration/bdd/high-level naming and inspected actual code paths.
- Confirmed six tests in that scope currently perform DCS-affecting control actions from tests:
  1) tests/bdd_api_http.rs::bdd_api_post_switchover_writes_dcs_key
  2) tests/bdd_api_http.rs::bdd_api_ha_routes_mutate_dcs_and_expose_state_contract
  3) tests/bdd_api_http.rs::bdd_api_ha_admin_routes_require_legacy_auth_token
  4) src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs::e2e_multi_node_real_ha_scenario_matrix
  5) src/ha/worker.rs::integration_transitions_replica_candidate_primary_and_failsafe
  6) src/ha/worker.rs::integration_primary_split_brain_enters_fencing_and_process_feedback_advances
- Also clarified in final report: strict HA-steering subset is 3 (e2e matrix + 2 ha/worker integration tests), while broader high-level DCS mutation scope is 6 including API BDD route tests.
- No codebase fixes, no test execution, no non-.ralph file modifications.
- Next step if asked: convert findings into task(s) for de-steering remaining HA tests away from direct DCS-driving control paths.

- Received PO follow-up email asking why writable `/ha/leader` exists, how it is accessible despite lease expectations, and to create a bug task to remove this ability and migrate tests to HA-loop behavior.
- Followed mail workflow with frequent updates: sent immediate ack, plan update, subtask start update, detailed findings update, and final completion summary.
- Performed read-only investigation:
  - traced routing and auth in `src/api/worker.rs` (`POST/DELETE /ha/leader` are admin endpoints; if no tokens configured auth allows all routes).
  - traced controller writes in `src/api/controller.rs` (routes directly call `DcsHaWriter::write_leader_lease/delete_leader`).
  - traced DCS store implementation in `src/dcs/store.rs` and `src/dcs/etcd_store.rs` (leader write uses plain etcd `put(..., None)` without lease options).
  - traced historical rationale in Ralph tasks 22/23/25 showing endpoint was intentionally introduced as full admin write surface and used by CLI/e2e control flows.
  - confirmed config has `ha.lease_ttl_ms` validation in parser but no corresponding etcd lease attachment usage in write path.
- Used add-bug skill and created bug task file:
  - `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-remove-writable-ha-leader-api-and-ha-loop-test-steering.md`
  - bug includes explicit scope to remove writable `/ha/leader` runtime+CLI paths and migrate tests/e2e to HA-loop-driven transitions with policy guard reinforcement.
- No runtime/src code changes were made; only `.ralph/tasks/bugs/` file creation/rename and mail/progress artifacts.
- Next if requested: execute the new bug task or split additional bug for true etcd lease attachment/renewal semantics.
